title: CLAUDE.md
text: Framework: llegir R package utilizing ellmer LLM orchestration. Direct Imports: Core tools must depend strictly ModuleSet adapter, never directly hdWGCNA Seurat. Contracts: Every tool output must strictly validate evidence_fragment schema. Boilerplate Prose: Programmatic synthesis outputs must strictly match interpretation schema. free-form prose generation. Style Rules: Follow STYLE.md exactly (tidyverse, snake_case, 4-space indent, single quotes, roxygen comments unless explicitly asked).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Environment: Always use active hdWGCNA conda environment. install new packages. Iteration run offline using mock backend response cache. live validation, iterate using exactly ONE module. call full pipeline syntheses. Default development provider Github (ellmer::chat_github(model = 'gpt-4o-mini') Google Gemini chat_google_gemini(model = 'gemini-1.5-flash')). Never prompt API keys.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: highly concise. Skip conversational filler, pleasantries, framing (e.g., say “Certainly, let’s write spec” “can help ”). explanations/summaries: Deliver code, scripts, markdown updates, specs directly zero wrapping explanatory text. Limit output prose: Keep chat responses 3 sentences unless specifically asked explain reasoning. ## Git & Commit Guidelines (Strict) Automated Commits: asked commit code, always use highly detailed, descriptive title body. Commit Format: Use Conventional Commits style (e.g., feat: ..., fix: ..., refactor: ...). Self-Attribution: add “Co-authored-: Claude”, “Created AI”, text referring contributor, assistant, author. commit message must read purely though written human developer. add conversational wrap-ups like “Everything working great!”. Commit Body: Explicitly list new functions added, modified data contracts, newly passed test files body commit message. Example:
code: feat: implement format-specific evidence ingestion runners   - Added format-specific column mappings for Seurat, DESeq2, and edgeR in import_fragment.R   - Added hdWGCNA DME table mappings to state_expression fragments   - Updated tests in test-import_fragment.R; all 14 tests passing.```

what: Setup data and define the ModuleSet
title: Getting started with llegir
text: vignette, demonstrate llegir using scRNA-seq dataset myeloid cells cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) cells profiled across several CNS conditions, co-expression modules computed hdWGCNA. start llegir pipeline, first define ModuleSet, contains following attributes: modules(), gene_membership(), module_scores(), expression(), metadata(), capabilities(), pkg_versions(). function hdWGCNA_ModuleSet() automatically formats ModuleSet object hdWGCNA results, framework flexible modules packages. quick look two attributes tools lean : ranked gene membership module cell-level metadata.
code: library(Seurat) library(hdWGCNA) library(tidyverse) library(llegir)  # load the CSF dataset, which has already been processed with hdWGCNA seurat_obj <- readRDS('data/CSF_Myeloid_hdWGCNA.rds')  # initialize the ModuleSet  ms <- hdWGCNA_ModuleSet(seurat_obj) modules(ms) head(gene_membership(ms, 'MM1')) head(metadata(ms))

title: Getting started with llegir
text: diving individual modules, llegir first gathers global context whole experiment, , via small toolbox dataset tools – kind standardized, evidence-emitting functions per-module tools , just scoped whole ModuleSet instead one module. dataset_composition_tool() summarizes cell-state census covariate balance (e.g. one cell state skewed across diagnosis groups?), dataset_variance_structure_tool() regresses principal component pseudo-bulk expression matrix declared metadata covariates, synthesis can tell whether module’s cross-condition signal reflects real biology technical axis (batch, sequencing depth) riding PC. run_dataset_context() mirrors run_orchestrator() , runs tool list whole ModuleSet rather per module: dataset_variance_structure_tool() needs pseudo-bulk view data (see pseudobulk_ModuleSet() / with_pseudobulk()) regress , skips gracefully – rather erroring – ms doesn’t carry one, . resulting dataset_ctx passed run_synthesis_orchestrator() (synthesize_module()) alongside desc, via dataset_context = dataset_ctx, injecting DATASET CONTEXT block every module’s synthesis prompt (see ## Interpreting evidence section ).
code: dataset_tool_config <- list(     list(fn = dataset_composition_tool, params = list(group_col = 'lv2_annot', condition_col = 'diagnosis')),     list(fn = dataset_variance_structure_tool, params = list(         covariates = c('diagnosis', 'nUMI'), condition_col = 'diagnosis'     )) )  dataset_ctx <- run_dataset_context(ms, dataset_tool_config) dataset_ctx$dataset_fragments[[1]]$compact_summary

what: Orchestrating a chain of analysis tools
title: Getting started with llegir
text: module characterized running set analysis tools. Every tool takes inputs (ModuleSet, module, parameters, bundled together ctx) returns one evidence_fragment: standardized summary tool found. demonstrate , run single tool see produces: llegir ships several pre-built analysis tools: top_genes_tool(): top genes kME cluster_dme_tool(): differential module expression across clusters geneset_enrichment_tool(): GO/pathway enrichment signature_correlation_tool(): co-variation signature library Additionally, external results already computed elsewhere can leveraged way via import_fragment(). run chain tools module set using run_orchestrator(), collects results one evidence packet per module, saved JSON file. tool list pairs tool (fn) parameters, modules_use limits run three modules example: packet holds evidence fragments plus packet_hash, fingerprint evidence contains. evidence always produces hash, makes runs easy track reproduce.
code: # set up the context (ctx) for the tool ctx <- list(ms = ms, module_id = 'MM1', params = list(n_hubs = 10))  # run the top genes tool to get the top 10 genes by membership top_frag <- top_genes_tool(ctx)  # sanity check: validate_evidence_fragment(top_frag)  # show the result top_frag$compact_summary modules_use <- c('MM1', 'MM2', 'MM3')  tool_config <- list(     list(fn = top_genes_tool, params = list(n_hubs = 25)),     list(fn = cluster_dme_tool, params = list(group_by = 'lv2_annot')),     list(fn = geneset_enrichment_tool, params = list(         n_hubs = 25,         db_files = c(             GO_BP = 'data/GO_Biological_Process_2026.txt',             Hallmark = 'data/h.all.v2026.1.Hs.symbols.gmt'         )     )) )  packets <- run_orchestrator(ms, tool_config, output_dir = 'output/evidence_packets', modules_use = modules_use) packet <- packets[['MM1']] packet$packet_hash length(packet$fragments)

text: interpreting modules, provide brief description dataset dataset_description(). biological context given model interprets module appropriate setting (gene program can mean something different CSF myeloid cells solid tumor). required: llegir run synthesis without .
code: desc <- dataset_description(     species = 'human',     tissue = 'cerebrospinal fluid (CSF)',     cell_compartment = 'myeloid cells',     assay = 'single-cell RNA-seq (10x)',     conditions = c(         'Glioblastoma', 'Brain Metastasis', 'Primary CNS lymphoma',         'Secondary CNS lymphoma', 'Inflammatory / other neuroinflammatory'     ),     notes = 'Modules are CSF-myeloid co-expression programs; interpret in a CNS-myeloid, neuro-oncology / neuroinflammation context.' ) cat(render_dataset_description(desc))

title: Getting started with llegir
text: run_synthesis_orchestrator() turns evidence packets written interpretations. module’s evidence packet sent user-specified LLM, checks every claim actually backed evidence (enforce_faithfulness()), combines model’s confidence evidence-based confidence (fuse_confidence()). saves interpretation readable Markdown paragraph per module, plus review_queue.tsv manifest.json whole run. language model’s role deliberately limited: ever sees summarized evidence, never raw data tables, never runs analysis . can use different model providers resolve_backend() ('github'; 'gemini' also works), reads API key environment (e.g. GITHUB_PAT GEMINI_API_KEY). Wrapping cached_backend() saves result disk, re-running synthesis unchanged packets doesn’t spend another API call. Inspect one interpretation: proposed label, fused confidence, rendered paragraph. review queue (build_review_queue()) collects interpretations flagged closer look (needs_review()), sorted lowest-confidence first, know modules check hand:
code: backend <- cached_backend(     resolve_backend('github'),     provider = 'github',     model = 'gpt-4o-mini',     prompt_template_version = PROMPT_TEMPLATE_VERSION )  interps <- run_synthesis_orchestrator(     packets, desc, backend = backend, output_dir = 'output/interpretations', dataset_context = dataset_ctx ) interp <- interps[['MM1']] interp$proposed_label interp$confidence$score cat(render_paragraph(interp)) build_review_queue(interps)

what: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
text: fixed set rules governing backend must fill model-facing interpretation schema: evidence-claims, citation requirements, direction consistency, controlled vocabularies, empty-literature constraint, confidence.score must relate deterministic EVIDENCE CONFIDENCE MATRIX (calculate_fusion_score()) injected user prompt build_user_prompt().

title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
code: build_system_prompt()

title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
text: single character string.

title: Build the synthesis system prompt — build_system_prompt
code: cat(build_system_prompt()) #> You are filling a structured interpretation of one gene co-expression module #> from a fixed evidence packet produced by a deterministic analysis pipeline. #>  #> Rules: #> - Use only the evidence given below. Do not invent genes, terms, or results, and do not run or imagine any analysis. #> - Every entry in supporting_claims must cite the fragment_id(s) it is based on, and its direction must match the direction reported by those fragments. #> - A single supporting_claims entry may only cite fragment_ids that all share the same direction. ranked_genes fragments (e.g. top_genes) always report direction na, so never combine one in the same claim as a directional fragment (e.g. geneset_enrichment, direction up/down) -- cite them as separate supporting_claims entries instead, each using the direction its own fragment(s) actually report. #> - metadata_associations entries must cite a real fragment_id the same way. #> - Each fragment has a type from a controlled vocabulary: ranked_genes, categorical_association, continuous_correlation, geneset_enrichment, signature_correlation, cross_condition_delta, state_expression. #> - flags must be drawn only from: insufficient_evidence, needs_human_review, possible_artifact, tool_conflict, label_low_specificity. #> - If the evidence is weak, sparse, or inconsistent, do not invent a confident story: set flags to include insufficient_evidence, keep supporting_claims minimal (or empty), and give a low confidence score. #> - The user prompt includes an EVIDENCE CONFIDENCE MATRIX computed deterministically upstream: treat it as ground truth, do not recompute or contradict it. #> - confidence.score must be consistent with the matrix's E_evidence: it may not exceed E_evidence + 0.10, and must fall below the matrix's stated insufficient_evidence threshold when E_evidence does. #> - Every quantitative certainty statement in your response must reference E_evidence rather than restating your own separate estimate. #> - You may not assert a direction (e.g. in dominant_biology or condition_dynamics) that contradicts the sign of the directional coherence reported in the matrix. #> - literature must be left empty; literature grounding is not available in this pipeline. #> - A DATASET CONTEXT block, when present, is global framing (composition, variance structure, and similar dataset-wide context) for confounder awareness -- it is not a per-module fragment, so never cite it in supporting_claims or metadata_associations.

what: Calculate the deterministic fused evidence confidence for a packet — calculate_fusion_score
title: Calculate the deterministic fused evidence confidence for a packet — calculate_fusion_score
text: Normalizes every fragment onto unified [0, 1] scale (.fragment_score(): type-aware magnitude link times significance- reliability factor), pools weighted power mean (.pool_evidence()) using tool-tier + user_weights importance (.tool_weight()), applies mass-weighted directional-conflict penalty (.directional_coherence()). Computed straight packet synthesis, result reproducible packet hash alone; injected synthesis prompt ground truth (.render_confidence_matrix()) re-used fuse_confidence() evidence term, printed fusion string can never drift math.

title: Calculate the deterministic fused evidence confidence for a packet — calculate_fusion_score
code: calculate_fusion_score(   fragments,   user_weights = list(),   beta = 0.5,   lambda = 0.35,   kappa = 0.5 )

title: Calculate the deterministic fused evidence confidence for a packet — calculate_fusion_score
text: fragments list evidence_fragment objects (packet's fragments). user_weights Named list per-tool_id weight multipliers top structural tier base (see register_tool()'s tier argument). Default list() (tiers unmodified). beta Corroboration exponent power mean; lower conjunctive (single weak tool offset strong one), higher compensatory. Default 0.5. lambda Model-trust weight final geometric blend (fuse_confidence()); threaded provenance returned list. Default 0.35. kappa Maximum directional-conflict penalty. Default 0.5.

title: Calculate the deterministic fused evidence confidence for a packet — calculate_fusion_score
text: list: e_evidence (final empirical evidence term), e_pool (pooled evidence directional penalty), p_agree (directional penalty factor), c_dir (directional coherence), lambda, params (beta, kappa), matrix (per-fragment data.frame prompt rendering audit).

title: Compute deterministic evidence signals from a packet — compute_evidence_signals
text: Signals independent model output: maximum bounded effect strength across fragments, number significant enrichment terms, cross-tool agreement whether module shows real signal. Two guards known false-positive patterns (thresholds tuned one dataset): fragment counts "signal" clears significance effect-size bar (large-N tests can hit p~0 practically negligible effect), enrichment term counts significant least min_overlap_genes overlapping genes (single-gene overlap narrow term classic false positive small hub-gene list).

text: trust prior every downstream cross_condition_delta: runs PCA pseudo-bulk expression matrix, regresses principal component declared metadata covariate (lm(pc_scores ~ covariate)), reporting R2/adj_R2/fdr per covariate x PC pair. Ported sample_code/pseudobulk_functions.R PseudobulkPCA() / PCRegression() (multi-covariate branch); see .pseudobulk_pca() / .pc_regression().

text: ctx$params$method selects statistic: 'limma' (default) fits limma::lmFit() / limma::eBayes() full module_scores(pb_view) matrix – variance moderation borrows strength across every module – extracts current module's row; fit cached (content hash module-score matrix design) across per-module orchestrator loop, runs exactly per dataset, per module. 'nonparametric' reuses categorical_group_test() (Kruskal-Wallis + one-vs-rest Wilcoxon, rank-biserial) directly module's pseudo-bulk scores, per module, cross-module step.

title: Fuse model and deterministic confidence into a final score and flags — fuse_confidence
text: model's self-reported confidence (interp$confidence$model_score) never trusted alone. blended calculate_fusion_score()'s deterministic e_evidence via weighted geometric blend (model_score^lambda * e_evidence^(1 - lambda)), blend can trigger review flags – fluent, confident label weak evidence gets caught even model never flags . fusion calculate_fusion_score() result already injected synthesis prompt (see R/prompt.R), printed fusion string can never drift model shown; passing NULL recomputes packet user_weights. Mutates returns interp: confidence$score overwritten (model_score preserved audit), flags unioned whatever model enforce_faithfulness() already set.

what: llegir: deterministic evidence and model-assisted interpretation of gene co-expression modules — llegir-package
title: llegir: deterministic evidence and model-assisted interpretation of gene co-expression modules — llegir-package
text: Loads gene co-expression module object (hdWGCNA first), gathers standardized bundle evidence per module via toolbox deterministic R functions, drafts evidence-backed interpretation paragraph via model-agnostic synthesis layer faithfulness checking, confidence fusion, confidence-gated human review. deterministic evidence core model dependency; synthesis backends pluggable (offline mock backend, live structured-output backends via 'ellmer'). title: A small, self-contained synthetic ModuleSet for examples and the vignette — llegir_example_moduleset

Runs fully offline deterministically (fixed seed), safe use @examples, tests, package vignette without real dataset.

title: Evidence tool: module score vs. a declared metadata column — module_by_metadata_tool
text: core evidence tool. Touches ModuleSet adapter contract (module_scores(), metadata(), pkg_versions()) plus shared categorical_group_test() / continuous_correlation_test() helpers. Categorical columns (e.g. diagnosis, sample) get group means plus Kruskal/one-vs-rest Wilcoxon; continuous columns get Pearson/Spearman correlation.

what: Build a ModuleSet from pseudo-bulk counts, re-scoring modules with decoupleR — pseudobulk_ModuleSet
title: Build a ModuleSet from pseudo-bulk counts, re-scoring modules with decoupleR — pseudobulk_ModuleSet
text: Ingests user-supplied pseudo-bulk counts matrix – llegir build pseudo-bulk – re-scores module definitions directly , rather averaging cell-level module scores. Scoring runs log2-CPM normalization counts via decoupleR::run_ulm(): gene's weight gene_table (e.g. hdWGCNA kME) becomes decoupleR's mode--regulation (mor) present, otherwise every gene gets uniform mor = 1. Builds components_ModuleSet() data_level = 'pseudobulk' aggregated = TRUE, validate_moduleset() passes counts()/capabilities()$counts populated.

text: tool function(ctx) -> evidence_fragment (NULL, skip; see run_module()). Registering makes runnable tool_config id (list(id = 'my_tool', params = list(...))) lets run_module() check required ModuleSet capabilities() calling , skipping gracefully recording packet unmet, rather tool self-skip. See docs/custom_tools.md worked template.

title: Register a tool (core or custom) in the tool registry — register_tool
text: id unique tool id, e.g. 'top_genes' 'my_custom_tool'. fn function(ctx) -> evidence_fragment, ctx list(ms, module_id, params) (see run_module()). type One evidence_fragment controlled vocabulary (see evidence_fragment()) tool may emit. Descriptive – fragment tool actually returns always checked full contract validate_evidence_fragment() regardless declared . tool whose emitted type depends params can declare one. description one-line, human-readable description tool . requires ModuleSet capabilities() tool needs run: either character vector (e.g. c('grouping', 'module_scores')), function(params) -> character vector tool whose requirement depends called. Default character(0) (requirement). tier Structural importance tier consulted calculate_fusion_score() weight tool's fragments: one 'high', 'medium', 'low'. Default 'medium'. Ignored scope == 'dataset' – dataset fragments never enter fusion. scope Whether tool runs per module (default) per dataset: one 'module', 'dataset'. 'module' tool's type checked evidence_fragment() vocabulary; 'dataset' tool's type dataset_fragment() vocabulary. Default 'module'.

what: Run a configured set of evidence tools over every module in a ModuleSet — run_orchestrator
title: Run a configured set of evidence tools over every module in a ModuleSet — run_orchestrator
text: Runs run_module() independently module modules_use (every module ms given) writes validated, hashed evidence packet output_dir JSON. One module's failure (e.g. tool erroring degenerate module) take whole batch; failures reported via warning() recorded NULL returned list. tables_dir, given, also persists every fragment's result table (via write_fragment_tables()) alongside JSON packets.

text: ms ModuleSet. tool_config list list(fn, params) specs; see run_module(). output_dir Directory write one evidence packet JSON file per module. tables_dir Optional directory also persist every fragment's result table. modules_use Optional subset module ids run; defaults modules ms. input_hash Optional hash input ModuleSet, recorded packet. module_method Optional free-form description modules generated; see run_module() / dataset_description(). Passed every run_module() call. Default NA. validate TRUE (default), run validate_moduleset() ms anything else, malformed adapter fails loudly front instead partway batch.

what: sentit: deterministic evidence and model-assisted interpretation of gene co-expression modules — sentit-package
title: sentit: deterministic evidence and model-assisted interpretation of gene co-expression modules — sentit-package
text: Loads gene co-expression module object (hdWGCNA first), gathers standardized bundle evidence per module via toolbox deterministic R functions, drafts evidence-backed interpretation paragraph via model-agnostic synthesis layer faithfulness checking, confidence fusion, confidence-gated human review. deterministic evidence core model dependency; synthesis backends pluggable (offline mock backend, live structured-output backends via 'ellmer'). Experimental: public API yet stable.

what: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: co-variation sibling geneset_enrichment_tool(): overlap asks whether module contains signature's genes; tool asks whether module's activity co-varies signature's. Scores every signature ctx$params$library_files across cells via UCell::ScoreSignatures_UCell() decoupleR::run_ulm() (scoring gene_list_ModuleSet() uses), correlates signature's score module's module_scores().

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
code: signature_correlation_tool(ctx)

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: ctx tool context list: list(ms, module_id, params), built run_module(). ctx$params$library_files (required) named character vector local .gmt .rds signature-library file paths, e.g. c(Hallmark = 'data/h..v2026.1.Hs.symbols.gmt'). .rds file must contain named list character vectors (gene sets); .gmt read via fgsea::gmtPathways(). ctx$params$method 'UCell' (default) 'decoupleR'.

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: evidence_fragment type 'signature_correlation', NULL ctx$ms lacks module_scores expression capability (see capabilities()) – graceful skip, error.

title: Evidence tool: correlate module activity with a signature library — signature_correlation_tool
text: Reports Pearson r descriptive co-variation cell level always (never p-value – cells independent). pseudobulk_view() resolves pseudo-bulk view ctx$ms (either ctx$ms pseudo-bulk ModuleSet, one attached via with_pseudobulk()), signature library re-scored view's expression correlated module_scores() instead, real p-value independent pseudo-bulk units. pseudo-bulk view available, descriptive cell-level r reported.

what: Synthesize one evidence packet into an interpretation via a backend — synthesize_interpretation
title: Synthesize one evidence packet into an interpretation via a backend — synthesize_interpretation
text: model's role bounded: fills model-facing schema (model_output_schema_json()) compact prompt built build_system_prompt() / build_user_prompt(); never sees raw evidence result tables never runs analysis code. module_id always taken packet, never trusted model's output, literature always forced empty (scope milestone regardless backend returns).

title: Synthesize one evidence packet into an interpretation via a backend — synthesize_interpretation
code: synthesize_interpretation(   packet,   desc,   backend,   temperature = 0,   seed = NA_real_,   prompt_template_version = PROMPT_TEMPLATE_VERSION,   schema_path = system.file("schemas", "interpretation.schema.json", package = "llegir"),   user_weights = list(),   fusion = NULL,   dataset_context = NULL )

title: Synthesize one evidence packet into an interpretation via a backend — synthesize_interpretation
text: packet evidence packet, built build_evidence_packet(). desc dataset_description; see dataset_description(). Required (hard error via validate_dataset_description() missing/empty). backend backend function; see mock_backend() contract. temperature Sampling temperature passed backend. seed Optional seed, recorded interpretation's provenance. prompt_template_version Prompt template version record interpretation's provenance. schema_path Path interpretation JSON schema; defaults schema shipped package. user_weights Named list per-tool_id weight multipliers passed calculate_fusion_score() EVIDENCE CONFIDENCE MATRIX injected user prompt (see build_user_prompt()). Default list(). fusion optional pre-computed calculate_fusion_score() result inject user prompt; NULL (default) computes packet$fragments user_weights. synthesize_module() computes passes object fuse_confidence(), prompt final fused score guaranteed agree. dataset_context optional dataset context, built build_dataset_context() / run_dataset_context(), threaded build_user_prompt(). NULL (default) omits DATASET CONTEXT block.

title: Synthesize one evidence packet into an interpretation via a backend — synthesize_interpretation
text: interpretation object (yet faithfulness-checked confidence-fused; see synthesize_module() full pipeline).

title: llegir 0.0.0.9000
text: Added pseudobulk_ModuleSet(): builds ModuleSet user-supplied pseudo-bulk counts matrix + metadata, SummarizedExperiment (SummarizedExperiment Suggests dependency), re-scoring module definitions directly pseudo-bulk matrix via decoupleR::run_ulm() (kME-style gene weights map mor present, else uniform mor = 1). Realized data_level = 'pseudobulk', aggregated = TRUE components_ModuleSet; validate_moduleset() passes. Added attachment API – with_pseudobulk(), pseudobulk(), pseudobulk_view() – pseudo-bulk view can ride alongside cell-level ModuleSet (run_orchestrator()) run standalone. Removed module_by_metadata_tool() (registry entry) along aggregate_by_sample(), is_sample_constant(), continuous_correlation_test() stats_utils.R. Sample-level inference module activity now belongs pseudo-bulk ModuleSet layer rather averaging correlated per-cell scores; categorical-association role replaced differential_module_activity_tool() (), run pseudo-bulk view. signature_correlation_tool() longer aggregates per-cell scores sample level . now resolves pseudo-bulk view via pseudobulk_view(ms): one attached (ms pseudobulk_ModuleSet), signature library re-scored view’s expression correlated re-scored module activity, real p-value independent pseudo-bulk units. pseudo-bulk view available, reports descriptive cell-level Pearson r , without p-value. Sample-level signature_correlation numbers differ prior runs design. Added differential_module_activity_tool(), module-level DME successor: tests whether module’s re-scored pseudo-bulk activity differs across declared contrast_col, independent pseudo-bulk samples via pseudobulk_view(). Emits cross_condition_delta fragment two-level contrast, categorical_association multi-level factor. method = 'limma' (default) fits limma::lmFit()/eBayes() full module-score matrix – variance moderation borrows strength across every module – caching fit across per-module orchestrator loop; method = 'nonparametric' reuses categorical_group_test() per module. Skips gracefully (logged reason) pseudo-bulk view resolves contrast_col isn’t usable. Registered tool registry. Added pseudobulk_de_limma_tool(), gene-level complement: runs limma-voom module’s genes within pseudo-bulk raw counts (counts(pseudobulk_view(ms))), two-level contrast_col plus optional covariates low-count gene filtering. Emits cross_condition_delta fragment (one row per gene). Skips gracefully pseudo-bulk view resolves, view lacks counts capability, gene survives filter. Registered tool registry. Packaged deterministic evidence core synthesis layer installable, documented R package. behavior change pre-package sourced-functions codebase (Milestones 1-2): inputs produce evidence packets, interpretations, rendered paragraphs. Experimental: public API yet stable. ModuleSet adapter contract custom-tool registry expected evolve 1.0 release.
